title: Global Highway, Street and Bridge Construction Market Size/Share Worth USD 2,184.43 Billion by 2035 at a 4.8% CAGR: Custom Market Insights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth, SWOT Analysis)
author: Custom Market Insights
description: The global highway, street and bridge construction market is valued at USD 1,284.67 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 2,184.43 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 4.8%. Growth is driven by government infrastructure stimulus, urbanization, aging transportation networks, and technological advancements including modular construction and smart infrastructure. Asia Pacific leads the market, followed by North America.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed among key market players in a growing construction market with steady 4.8% CAGR and increasing government funding for infrastructure projects globally.
Key market player well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand for highway, street and bridge construction driven by urbanization and infrastructure modernization.

title: VINCI : Mise en œuvre du programme de rachat d’actions propres
author: Vinci Press Service
description: VINCI has signed an agreement with an investment service provider on June 10, 2026 to repurchase its own shares up to €200 million. The buyback program is valid from June 11 to July 31, 2026, with purchase prices not exceeding limits set by the general assembly.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Share buyback programs are typically viewed positively as they signal management confidence in the company's valuation, can support earnings per share, and demonstrate commitment to returning value to shareholders. This is a constructive capital allocation decision.

title: VINCI: Implementation of the share buyback programme
author: Na
description: VINCI signed a share purchase agreement on June 10th, 2026 with an investment services provider to repurchase its own shares up to €200 million between June 11th and July 31st, 2026. The buyback is authorized by the shareholders' meeting with a maximum purchase price limit.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Share buyback programmes are typically viewed positively as they signal management confidence in the company's valuation, can support earnings per share, and represent a commitment to returning value to shareholders. This is a constructive capital allocation decision.

title: Thierry Mirville nommé directeur financier de VINCI
author: Vinci Press Service
description: Thierry Mirville has been appointed as Chief Financial Officer of VINCI effective June 1, 2026, succeeding Christian Labeyrie who is retiring. Mirville brings extensive financial experience from his 35-year career within VINCI and its subsidiaries, most recently serving as Deputy CFO and Executive Committee member since October 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The appointment of an internally promoted CFO with deep knowledge of the company's operations and 35 years of experience demonstrates strong leadership continuity and organizational stability. This internal succession planning is typically viewed positively by markets as it reduces transition risk and shows confidence in management development.

title: Thierry Mirville appointed Vice-President, Chief Financial Officer, VINCI
author: Vinci Press Department
description: Thierry Mirville has been appointed Vice-President and Chief Financial Officer of VINCI effective June 1, 2026, succeeding Christian Labeyrie's retirement. Mirville brings extensive experience from various CFO roles within VINCI subsidiaries and divisions since 1991.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The appointment of an experienced internal executive with a strong track record across multiple VINCI divisions to the CFO position demonstrates organizational stability and continuity in financial leadership. The succession planning is orderly and the appointee has proven expertise within the company.
